strengths: Selection of three distinct, real-world objects with clear contextual details
Explicit links made between each object and IA prompt #18, discussing facets of the âunknowableâ
Multiple claims supported by dates, statistics and cited sources
Concluding statement effectively contrasts how each object embodies different dimensions of the prompt
Clear statement of the selected IA prompt at the outset
weaknesses: Explanations sometimes lack precision and conflate concepts (e.g., chaos theory vs determinism)
Speculative assertions (e.g., Chinese cover-up) are not substantiated by evidence
Connections to TOK concepts (ways of knowing, knowledge questions) are implicit rather than explicit
Repetition and descriptive passages reduce analytical depth
No explicit knowledge question to frame the exhibitionâs final reflection
Inconsistent citation formatting and minor presentation errors
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work clearly meets the descriptors for the 5â6 band: three specific objects are identified with real-world contexts, and links to the IA prompt are explained and supported by evidence. The student provides meaningful justification for each objectâs inclusion and demonstrates a coherent overall argument. However, imprecision in explanations, speculative reasoning without corroboration, occasional lack of explicit TOK terminology and the absence of an articulated knowledge question prevent the work from reaching the 7â8 band. A score of 6 accurately reflects a strong exhibition that nonetheless has notable gaps in clarity, rigour and critical depth.

annotations.comment: The term âchaoticâ is introduced without linking to a specific way of knowing; explicitly connect chaos theory to sense perception or reason to align with TOK frameworks.
The concluding statement effectively presents the provisional nature of knowledge and contrasts how each object embodies different facets of the âunknowable.â
Ensure each online source includes both author, title, publication year, and access date in a consistent order to enhance academic rigour.
The references section is formatted properly, but citation details for Ford are missing a publication date; ensure consistency with a recognized style guide.
The speculative theory about a Chinese cover-up is intriguing but lacks corroborating evidence; indicate source or clearly frame it as conjecture.
When discussing politics and nationalism, explicitly link back to the prompt by explaining how withholding the film exemplifies âunknowableâ in historical knowledge.
The conclusion lacks an explicit knowledge question to tie the insights together; consider formulating one to frame the exhibitionâs final reflection.
The sentence ends mid-thought (âandâ); ensure statements are complete for coherence and full exposition of the argument.
The student clearly states the selected IA prompt (#18) at the outset, establishing a focused context for the exhibition.
The introduction lacks a concise thesis outlining how the three objects will collectively address the prompt; consider summarizing the overarching argument early.
Online source URLs are included, yet access dates are inconsistent; verify that each reference follows uniform formatting for reliability.
The student effectively explains the physical characteristics and manufacturing standards of a fair die, providing clear real-world context.
The discussion conflates chaos theory with determinism; in TOK, unpredictability (chaos) differs conceptually from philosophical determinism. Clarify this distinction.
The object title is clear, but a brief preview here of its connection to âunknowableâ would better orient the reader before the analysis begins.
Mathematical notation appears as raw code; format the probability properly as $$\frac{1}{6}$$ to meet presentation standards.
The analysis lacks a critical TOK concept: consider posing a knowledge question such as âTo what extent can we predict random phenomena?â to deepen the argument.
Strength: The student provides publication details and author background, grounding the book in its real-world context.
Sales figures illustrate popularity but the link to âunknowableâ is not established; explicitly justify why widespread readership relates to the prompt.
The historical context of Mallory and Irvine is well-established, supporting the relevance of the camera. Effective use of specific factual details.

The claim that Fordâs legitimacy should be questioned is asserted rather than analyzed; develop this into a knowledge question, e.g., âHow does absence of direct experience affect reliable shared knowledge?â.
Ensure each assertion is supported by TOK terminology; discuss how emotion and personal knowledge interact in shaping claims to know.
The conclusion succinctly contrasts the three objects, highlighting different dimensions of âunknowable.â Effective comparative summary.
The conclusion introduces the idea of provisional knowledge but could be deepened by referencing TOK concepts like paradigm shifts or the fallibility of memory.
The conclusion does not pose a knowledge question; adding one would strengthen the exhibitionâs final reflection and connect all sections clearly.
Citations are present, but the reference list lacks consistent styling; adopt a uniform format (e.g., APA or MLA) for all entries.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
